headline: AMD Jumps 7%, Intel Climbs 5%, Broadcom Rises 2% as Tom Lee Calls the Chip Selloff a Buying Opportunity

headline: The Motor Driving AMD’s Stock Rise Is Set to Stall
summary: Advanced Micro Devices  has soared over the past year amid excitement over its central-processing units.  AMD shares were up 1.6% at $526.07 in Thursday’s premarket, adding to a 259% gain over the past 12 months through to Wednesday’s close.  “AMD’s 146% surge since April, versus 58% for the SOX [PHLX Semiconductor Index], has left the shares priced at a premium to peers with little room for error,” Naji wrote.

content.title: The Motor Driving AMD’s Stock Rise Is Set to Stall
content.summary: Advanced Micro Devices  has soared over the past year amid excitement over its central-processing units.  AMD shares were up 1.6% at $526.07 in Thursday’s premarket, adding to a 259% gain over the past 12 months through to Wednesday’s close.  “AMD’s 146% surge since April, versus 58% for the SOX [PHLX Semiconductor Index], has left the shares priced at a premium to peers with little room for error,” Naji wrote.

headline: U.S. chip stocks rebound as Meta plans to double GW capacity, China semis rally
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. chip stocks bounced back Thursday, recovering some of the ground lost earlier in the week, after a rally in Chinese semiconductor shares helped steady investor nerves following a selloff following Samsung Electronics’ latest results. Additionally, fears of an AI CapEx slowdown eased after Reuters reported that Meta Platforms plans to double its computing power to 14 gigawatts next year and expects its custom AI chip to start production in September.

content.title: U.S. chip stocks rebound as Meta plans to double GW capacity, China semis rally
content.summary: Investing.com -- U.S. chip stocks bounced back Thursday, recovering some of the ground lost earlier in the week, after a rally in Chinese semiconductor shares helped steady investor nerves following a selloff following Samsung Electronics’ latest results. Additionally, fears of an AI CapEx slowdown eased after Reuters reported that Meta Platforms plans to double its computing power to 14 gigawatts next year and expects its custom AI chip to start production in September.
